DXY (US Dollar Index) Analysis

DXY and gold typically move inversely — a strengthening dollar pressures gold prices. Win rate is shown per DXY regime at each trade entry date (using 1D DXY data).

DXY × XAUUSD Return Correlation

30-day rolling correlation between DXY and XAUUSD daily returns. Avg: -0.467 (negative = inverse relationship, as expected for gold).

Multi-Timeframe (MTF) Analysis

Each 30m entry is tagged with the nearest higher-timeframe (60m, 4H, 1D) RSI context. State definition: bullish = RSI > RSI-MA and RSI-MA slope rising; bearish = RSI < RSI-MA and RSI-MA slope falling; neutral = mixed signals.

Data coverage:
trades_covered total_trades coverage_pct
timeframe
60m 57 161 35.400
4H 151 161 93.800
1D 161 161 100.000

⚠️ Counter-trend entries (4H bearish) have a 13.7% lower win rate than aligned entries (38.7% vs 52.4%). Consider filtering out entries when 4H RSI is in a bearish state.
